claim: Evidence schema, prompt, readiness contract, and tests all distinguish project, external, user, and unknown evidence scopes.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION defines evidence schema with 'scope is project | external | user | unknown' and explicitly states 'project facts require project-scoped evidence.'
evidence.why: This is the authoritative schema definition that establishes the four valid scope values.
Explicit constant validates only these four scope values are accepted throughout the codebase.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope() function handles all four scopes explicitly: project (repo/repository/codebase/local), external (web/internet/research), user (human/operator), unknown (unverified). Falls back based on path/url/source patterns.
evidence.why: Core scope inference logic implements all four scope types with explicit detection rules.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordHasDeclaredScope() validates evidence records declare one of the four VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES.
evidence.why: Enforcement function ensures records explicitly declare valid scope from the four allowed values.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordHasProjectLocator() checks project-scoped records include a repo-relative file path, rejecting URLs.
evidence.why: Project scope enforcement requires concrete path locator, not external URLs.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() returns PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE error when evidence lacks project scope, and PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED when project-scoped records lack paths.
evidence.why: Readiness contract enforces project facts need project-scoped evidence with paths, not external sources.

evidence.summary: Agent prompt hard rules: 'Every evidence record must include scope: project, external, user, or unknown.' and 'Project facts require project-scoped evidence from code, tests, docs, config, logs, or artifacts, with a repo-relative path or file locator.'
evidence.why: Agent prompt explicitly requires all four scope values and links project facts to project-scoped evidence only.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeErrors() validates each evidence record declares scope as one of the four VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES.
evidence.why: Runtime validation in evidence-dispatch enforces scope requirement using the same four-scope constant.

evidence.summary: Test 'external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts' uses evidence with scope='external' and verifies PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blocker.
evidence.why: Test validates external scope is distinguished from project scope and cannot satisfy project facts.

evidence.summary: Test 'project evidence requires a concrete project locator' uses scope='project' evidence without path and verifies PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker.
evidence.why: Test validates project scope requires path locator.

evidence.summary: Test 'external URLs cannot be relabeled as project evidence' uses scope='project' with url field and verifies PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker.
evidence.why: Test validates project scope cannot be faked with external URLs.

evidence.summary: Test 'evidence records must declare valid scope explicitly' omits scope field and verifies EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED blocker.
evidence.why: Test validates evidence must explicitly declare one of the four scopes.

risks: If claim is wrong, PRD readiness could incorrectly accept external research as project facts, allowing web URLs to satisfy existing-project evidence requirements.

result.notes: Independent verification confirms all four components (schema, prompt, readiness contract, tests) explicitly distinguish and validate the four evidence scopes. Schema defines scopes in DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION, constants validate them in VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES, inference logic distinguishes all four in evidenceRecordScope(), readiness enforces project-evidence requirements in projectEvidenceBlockingIssues(), agent prompts require explicit declaration, and tests cover all validation paths including scope declaration, path requirements, and external-vs-project separation.
